title: The Great Rotation Has Crushed Growth Stocks. History Says That's Usually When You Should Be Buying Them.
author: John Ballard
description: Following the 'Great Rotation' that has shifted investor focus from growth to defensive stocks, the article argues this presents a buying opportunity in quality tech stocks. Nvidia, Microsoft, and Alphabet are highlighted as attractive investments with strong AI growth prospects and reasonable valuations, supported by historical data showing growth stocks outperform over long-term horizons.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Central to AI investment cycle with $1 trillion in cumulative purchase orders for chips, 71% revenue growth expected, 55% profit margin, and strong competitive moat. Trading at reasonable 18x forward earnings with 38% annual earnings growth forecast.

Down 30% from recent highs but showing strong fundamentals with 26% cloud revenue growth, 15 million paid Copilot seats, and 39% Azure growth. Positioned to extend productivity software dominance through AI infrastructure at attractive 22x forward earnings.

Generating strong double-digit advertising and cloud growth with $73 billion annual free cash flow. Gemini AI integration across products showing strong early results. Trading at reasonable 25x forward earnings with significant long-term growth potential.

Generating strong double-digit advertising and cloud growth with $73 billion annual free cash flow. Gemini AI integration across products showing strong early results. Trading at reasonable 25x forward earnings with significant long-term growth potential.
Historical data shows nearly doubled returns versus Value ETF over 10 years despite 12-24% drawdowns, supporting thesis that growth stocks are optimal for long-term investors with 10+ year horizons.

title: Calydon Capital Dumps $9 Million of ZoomInfo Amid Stock's 92% Decline Since 2021
author: Josh Kohn-Lindquist
description: Calydon Capital sold 892,298 shares of ZoomInfo Technologies (worth $9.19 million) in Q4 2025, reducing its position to just 0.14% of AUM. The sale reflects investor concern over ZoomInfo's stalled sales growth and 92% stock decline since 2021, though the company maintains strong free cash flow generation and growing large customer contracts.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock down 92% since 2021 with stalled sales growth. Fund manager Calydon Capital nearly liquidated its position, signaling loss of confidence. Despite strong FCF and large customer growth, lack of clear growth narrative and fierce competition in AI-powered market weigh heavily on investor sentiment.
Mentioned as a top holding of Calydon Capital (2.4% of AUM, $13.99 million) but no specific commentary provided. Neutral mention in context of fund's portfolio allocation.
Listed as Calydon Capital's largest holding at 7.4% of AUM ($43.42 million), indicating preference for broad market growth exposure over individual stock picks like ZoomInfo.
Second-largest holding at 5.6% of AUM ($32.57 million), reflecting fund's preference for diversified index exposure.

title: Can Growth ETFs Power Ahead as Optimism Builds?
author: Na
description: The current economic environment presents opportunities for growth-oriented investments, with the S&P 500 Growth Index outperforming value indexes. Factors like softer inflation, positive earnings season, and anticipated Fed rate cuts are fueling market optimism.

title: Fed Rate-Cut Chorus Grows Louder: Could This Be A Breakout Moment For Growth ETFs?
author: Chandrima Sanyal
description: Federal Reserve officials are signaling potential interest rate cuts this year due to slowing job growth, which could trigger a rally in growth-focused ETFs by reducing borrowing costs and attracting investors to equities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Potential beneficiary of lower interest rates as a large growth stock in ETF portfolios

Concentrated growth ETF likely to be sensitive to potential rate cut cycles

title: Insiders Chase Income and Stability in American Tower—Here’s Why
author: Marketbeat.Com
description: Insiders are buying shares of American Tower Corp (AMT) due to its stable income and price stability, which are attractive features in the current uncertain market environment. The stock has outperformed the S&P 500 and offers a dividend yield that beats inflation and Treasury bond yields.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Insiders are buying the stock due to its stable income and price stability, which are attractive features in the current uncertain market environment. The stock has outperformed the S&P 500 and offers a dividend yield that beats inflation and Treasury bond yields.
The article mentions that value stocks have fallen out of favor compared to growth stocks, indicating a potential rotation back into value stocks in the future.
The article suggests that growth stocks have become overextended and a rotation back into value stocks is likely to occur, which would negatively impact growth stocks.
